docs.summary: A picker for selecting items on a mobile app.
description: <blockquote class="important">
__Important:__
+ This API is only relevant to custom widgets built for [Branded Apps](https://www.wix.com/app-builder).
+ This API is in Alpha, and is not yet available to all users.
A picker for selecting items on a mobile app.

docs.summary: Sets or gets the picker's options.
description: Setting the `options` property sets all the options available to a mobile app user.
Getting the `options` property returns the current list of options available to a mobile app visitor.
You cannot modify the data array in-place. To add, change, or remove individual mobile picker options:
1. Store the value of the `options` property in a variable.
2. Make changes to the options array.
3. Reset the `options` property with the modified array.

examples.title: Get the picker's options
body: let myPickerOptions = $w('#mobilePicker1').options; // [{ value: 'L', label: 'Large', description: 'Adult Large', selected: false, enabled: true }, { value: 'XL', label: 'Extra Large', description: 'Adult Extra Large', selected: false, enabled: true }]

examples.title: Set the picker's options
body: { value: 'S', label: 'Small', description: 'Adult Small', selected: false, enabled: true },
{ value: 'M', label: 'Medium', description: 'Adult Medium', selected: false, enabled: true },
{ value: 'L', label: 'Large', description: 'Adult Large', selected: false, enabled: true },
{ value: 'XL', label: 'Extra Large', description: 'Adult Extra Large', selected: false, enabled: true }

docs.summary: Sets or gets the option dialog's subtitle.
Sets or gets the option dialog's title.
Sets or gets the indicies of the selected options.
description: Setting the `selectedIndices` property sets the options at each specified index to be a selected option.
To reset the mobile picker to have no option selected, set the `selectedIndices` property to an empty array, null, or `undefined`.
Getting the `selectedIndices` property returns an array of the indices of all selected options. If no value is selected, the `selectedIndices` property returns an empty array.

examples.title: Get the indices of the selected options
Set the indices of the selected options

docs.summary: Indicates if the mobile picker element's value is valid.
description: The `valid` property indicates if an element's value satisfies
all conditions to pass a validation check. This includes basic validity
conditions, such as whether the element has a value if it is [required]($w.RequiredMixin.html#required).
Note that validations other than **required**
are not run on the mobile picker element when they don't have a value.

examples.title: Get whether the element is valid

docs.summary: Sets or gets whether to validate the mobile element when its value changes.
description: If `validateOnChange` is `true`, the mobile element will validate when its value changes.
If `validateOnChange` is `false`, the mobile element won't validate when its value changes.

examples.title: Get whether to validate the mobile element on change
Set whether to validate the mobile element on change

docs.summary: Sets or gets whether to validate the mobile element when it is loaded.
description: If `validateOnStart` is `true`, the mobile element will validate when it is finished loading.
If `validateOnStart` is `false`, the mobile element won't validate when it is finished loading.

docs.summary: Sets or gets the validations for mobile elements in a form.
description: Setting the `validations` property sets the validations for mobile elements in a form.
Getting the `validations` property returns the current list of validation objects for mobile elements in a form.
Each validation object contains a `validation` and a `message` property.

examples.title: Get the mobile element's validations
body: let myValidations = $w('#mobilePicker1').validations; // [{ validation: 'required', message: 'This field is Required!' },{ validation: 'url', message: 'Not a valid URL.' }]

docs.summary: Sets or gets the value of the selected option.
description: Setting the `value` property sets the `options` property's value
to be the selected option. You must set the `value`
property to a value that exists in the [`options`](#options) list.
To reset the mobile picker to have no options
selected, set the `value` property to an empty string, `null` or `undefined`.
Getting the `value` property returns the value of
the currently selected option. If no value is selected, the
`value` property returns an empty string.
> **Notes:**
> + Changing a mobile picker's `value` in code does not trigger an `onChange` event.
> + If multiple options are currently selected, the `value` property returns the first selection. To get all the selected options, use the [`selectedIndices`](#selectedIndices) property.

examples.title: Get the value of the selected option
Set the selected option by value

params.name: handler
params.doc:
The name of the function or
 the function expression to run when the mobile picker element's value changes.

ret.doc: The mobile picker element on which the event is now registered.

docs.summary:
Adds an event handler that runs when the mobile picker element's value
 is changed.
description: An element receives a `change` event when a user changes the value
in the mobile picker element.
A `change` event is not triggered when you change the mobile picker element's value using
the element's [`value`](#value) property.
Because `onChange()` is fired before validations are performed, do not check
any of the validation properties in the `onChange()` event handler. For example,
do not code an if statement that checks the `valid` property in the `onChange()`
because the property at this point contains values prior to the `onChange()`.

examples.title: Get the value of the element that was changed
Enable the submit button when an option is selected

docs.summary: An object used by the `options` property that contains the attributes of a mobile picker item.

members.name: value
members.doc: The value of the mobile picker option. This is what you use in code and is what is stored in your collections. Mandatory if `label` is not specified.

members.name: label
members.doc: The label of the mobile picker option. This is what a mobile app user sees.  Mandatory if `value` is not specified.

members.name: description
members.doc: The description of the mobile picker option. This is what a mobile app user sees.

members.name: selected
members.doc: Whether the mobile picker option is selected.

members.name: enabled
members.doc: Whether the mobile picker option is enabled.
